title: Zenno Astronautics to relocate to the United States
summary: Zenno Astronautics, a New Zealand startup specializing in superconducting magnets for spacecraft, will relocate its headquarters to the United States to enhance its operations and market reach.

title: SpaceX launches Northrop mission to extend the life of aging satellites
summary: SpaceX launched a Northrop Grumman mission to extend the life of aging satellites by installing propulsion pods on three commercial satellites in geostationary orbit. The DARPA-backed vehicle aims to enhance satellite longevity and operational efficiency.

title: Northrop adds to charges on Vulcan solid rocket motor program
summary: Northrop Grumman incurred additional charges for the Vulcan Centaur's solid rocket boosters, with updated motors potentially delayed until year-end.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Three New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) has announced the addition of three new associate members: Overview Energy, Loft Orbital, and Star Catcher. These companies are involved in space-based energy and other space-related industries.

title: UK and Florida commit $400,000 to joint space projects
summary: The UK and Florida have each committed $200,000 to fund joint space projects focused on research, innovation, and commercialization. This collaboration aims to strengthen ties between the two regions in the space industry.

title: German component supplier deltaVision raises 10.2 million euros
summary: German spacecraft component developer deltaVision raised 10.2 million euros ($11.6 million) to expand production of valves and related equipment, focusing on enabling in-space refueling.

title: Agile Space Industries Appoints new CTO and Board member
summary: Agile Space Industries has appointed Jake Mills as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO) and Mark Pasquale as an independent board member. The appointments were announced on July 21, 2026, in Durango, Colorado.

title: KBR organizes defense technology business to pursue Golden Dome work
summary: KBR has restructured its defense technology business to focus on the Golden Dome program, leveraging its expertise in digital engineering, space tracking, and missile defense. The move aims to capitalize on opportunities in missile defense and space-related defense contracts.

title: IHI explores Kuva hyperspectral satellites for Japan’s multi-sensor constellation
summary: IHI is evaluating Kuva Space's next-generation hyperspectral satellites for Japan's planned 100-satellite Earth observation constellation. The Finnish startup's technology could enhance the multi-sensor sovereign satellite network.

title: Italian startup ORiS raises funding for laser power-beaming technology
summary: Italian startup ORiS has secured 5 million euros ($5.7 million) in funding to advance its laser-based wireless power-beaming technology for satellites. This innovation aims to enable energy transmission to spacecraft without physical connections.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers the secrets of cosmic 'factories' that filled the early universe with stardust
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has uncovered the secrets of early galaxies that produced stardust in the early universe. Astronomers studied a nearby modern galaxy to understand this cosmic phenomenon.

title: Alien life likely can't survive on exoplanets smaller than Mars, scientists say
summary: Scientists suggest that exoplanets smaller than Mars are unlikely to support alien life due to their inability to maintain a stable atmosphere. A planet at least the size of Mars is required for life to potentially emerge.

title: Space Force orders 36 more Golden Dome missile-tracking satellites, for $1.75 billion
summary: The Space Development Agency awarded contracts worth up to $1.75 billion to two companies for developing 36 Golden Dome missile-tracking satellites. This initiative supports the Trump administration's missile-defense program.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket sends Starlink satellites to orbit a day after rare launch abort
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched 24 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on July 21, following a rare launch abort the previous day.

title: Artemis III astronauts will beam 4k video from Orion to Earth using SpaceX Starlink lasers
summary: NASA will use SpaceX's Starlink laser communications terminals on the Artemis III Orion spacecraft to transmit 4K video and data-heavy mission downlinks to Earth. This partnership aims to enhance high-definition video transmissions during the mission.

title: The 'Avengers Doomsday' trailer is cool, but it's missing something... where's all the space stuff?
summary: The news humorously points out the lack of space-related content in the 'Avengers Doomsday' trailer, highlighting a missed opportunity for space themes in a major film.

title: Scientists may have found the 1st twin star system where both stars exploded
summary: Scientists have potentially discovered the first twin star system where both stars exploded as supernovae. This rare phenomenon could provide new insights into stellar evolution and cosmic events.

title: James Webb Space Telescope looks back in time 4.4 billion years | Space photo of the day for July 21, 2026
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured an image of a young galaxy cluster, MACS J0553.4-3342, looking back 4.4 billion years. This marks a significant milestone in deep-space observation.

title: SpaceX launches satellite repair drone with 10-foot robotic arms to Earth orbit (video)
summary: SpaceX launched Northrop Grumman's MRV-MEP satellite-servicing mission to Earth orbit, featuring a satellite repair drone with 10-foot robotic arms. This mission aims to extend the lifespan of existing satellites through in-space servicing.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman from Florida. This mission marks a significant step in robotic satellite servicing technology.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt at the Starship Flight 13 launch after a previous abort. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space are making progress on their respective medium-lift launch vehicles: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5. Despite the attention on SpaceX's Starship, these companies continue advancing their reusable rocket programs.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition, preventing a planned July 16 launch attempt. The launch was delayed due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission to maintain and support operations aboard the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th test flight. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. The telescope is now undergoing final preparations before its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on five decades of space exploration and innovation by the European Space Agency.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: The European Space Agency's PLATO spacecraft has completed electromagnetic compatibility tests in the Maxwell Test Chamber at ESTEC, confirming its electronics are fit for space. The mission is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket, scheduled for March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news discusses 'The art of hadrons' without providing additional context or details. It appears to be a title or headline without further information.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025. These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It emphasizes the innovation and significance of this technology in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will support expanding manufacturing capacity and advancing new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government allocated £62 million ($83.3 million) to enhance domestic satellite communication and space technology capabilities. This investment aims to strengthen the UK's position in the space industry.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace became the first private Indian company to reach orbit with its Vikram-1 rocket, lifting off from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the pool from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This expansion aims to bolster the National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has raised $10 million in a seed funding round and announced a move to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This marks a significant step in integrating commercial space technologies into NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the aftermath of ISU's liquidation and their vision for the future. The interview explores what led to the liquidation and potential next steps for the organization.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12 has raised a $70 million Series C funding round to scale its operations and meet global demand for sovereign space hardware. The European space company aims to expand its in-orbit capabilities.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns about the office's ability to manage the new framework.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has finalized its merger with Big Network to create a vertically integrated, multi-modal connectivity platform. The deal aims to enhance orchestration services in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The mission marks a significant step in satellite servicing and in-space logistics.

title: Arianespace Sets Date for Fourth Ariane 6 Launch This Year
summary: Arianespace has scheduled the fourth Ariane 6 launch of the year for August 27, which will carry the second Meteosat Third Generation Imager satellite (MTG-I2). This mission highlights Arianespace's ongoing efforts to deploy advanced weather monitoring satellites.

title: Poland Commits Nearly $750M to Europe’s IRIS² Constellation
summary: Poland has committed 656 million euros ($748 million) to Europe's IRIS² satellite constellation, covering nearly 10% of the project's funding. The agreement was signed in Warsaw.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government plans to invest an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, marking India's first private orbital launch. The mission achieved orbit, making it a historic milestone for the country's space industry.

title: ThinKom ThinAir GT Antenna Secures Government Certification for O3b mPower
summary: ThinKom's ThinAir GT2517 antenna has become the first airborne terminal to receive SES's Government Technology Certification for use on the O3b mPOWER constellation. This milestone enables government applications on SES's low Earth orbit satellite network.

title: Spire Hires Mantech Exec Eric Mellinger as COO
summary: Spire Global has appointed Eric Mellinger, a former Mantech International executive, as its Chief Operating Officer to oversee business development and go-to-market strategy. Mellinger brings nearly eight years of experience from Mantech International to his new role.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on lunar exploration, focusing on technical information exchange and system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin. The signals intelligence company participated in the multilateral training event.
